coreCompetencies: Basic grasp of machine learning fundamentals and how models learn from data
Elementary command of the linear algebra, calculus and probability used in AI
Ability to reproduce results from simple published experiments
Basic practice reading a research paper and extracting its contribution
Ability to run and log experiments with frameworks such as PyTorch
Elementary practice with experiment tracking and keeping results reproducible
Basic grasp of how deep learning models are trained and evaluated
Ability to document methods and findings clearly
complementaryCompetencies: Basic statistics and hypothesis testing
Familiarity with Python and scientific computing libraries
Elementary sense of the computing resources needed for training
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Can assist experienced researchers
Needs 1-2 years of foundational training

coreCompetencies: Ability to implement methods described in research papers from scratch
Ability to design controlled experiments with clear baselines
Practical command of neural network architectures and training dynamics
Familiarity with optimization methods and hyperparameter search
Ability to run ablation studies to isolate what drives results
Ability to conduct a structured literature review on a topic
Practice with reproducibility, fixed seeds and reporting variance
Grasp of research ethics and responsible experimentation
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with distributed and multi-GPU training
Ability to produce clear figures and result visualizations
Sense of statistical significance in experimental comparisons
indicators: Conducts experiments with supervision
Implements and validates recent techniques
Publishes at workshops or contributes to papers

coreCompetencies: Command of state-of-the-art methods in one or more research areas
Ability to formulate hypotheses and propose novel methods to test them
Ability to lead a focused research line end to end
Solid command of the theoretical foundations behind the methods used
Ability to collaborate with academic groups on joint work
Ability to review papers and give constructive technical feedback
Practice writing papers for conferences and workshops
Ability to mentor junior researchers on experimental design
complementaryCompetencies: Practice writing research proposals and grant applications
Ability to design benchmarks and evaluation protocols
Sense of open-sourcing code and datasets responsibly
indicators: Leads a research line with minimal supervision
Publishes at peer-reviewed conferences
Collaborates with academia and reviews for venues

coreCompetencies: Ability to define a research agenda for a team over several projects
Command of rigorous experimental methodology across studies
Ability to lead a small research team and coordinate their work
Solid command of the current landscape in the team's research areas
Ability to build collaborations with academic and industry partners
Ability to translate research findings toward product applications
Practice protecting and publishing intellectual property
Ability to secure funding through grants or internal proposals
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with foundation models and their training regimes
Ability to shape safety and alignment considerations in research
Sense of scaling laws and compute-efficient experimentation
indicators: Defines the direction of a research team
Leads strategic collaborations
Publishes influential peer-reviewed work

coreCompetencies: Expertise in building and leading a research group
Ability to run interdisciplinary research spanning multiple fields
Command of reproducibility and rigor standards across the group
Ability to form strategic partnerships with labs and universities
Solid command of the funding landscape and how to navigate it
Ability to define the research culture and review practices of a group
Expertise in developing research talent and career growth
Ability to measure and communicate the impact of research
complementaryCompetencies: Practice connecting research programs to product roadmaps
Ability to organize workshops and research events
Sense of publication strategy and venue selection
indicators: Architects research programs spanning several lines
Leads multiple research lines
Defines the research strategy of a group

coreCompetencies: Ability to define a multi-year research vision for an area
Expertise in producing sustained, high-impact research results
Command of building and maintaining external research networks
Ability to guide the transfer of research into products
Solid command of where a research field is heading
Ability to represent the organization in the scientific community
Expertise in shaping research programs and their evaluation
Ability to set ethical and safety standards for research work
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with cross-institution collaboration and consortia
Ability to advise on research investment decisions
Sense of long-horizon versus applied research trade-offs
indicators: Defines the research vision for an area
Leads research groups and programs
Influences the direction of a research field

coreCompetencies: Technical leadership of research strategy across an organization
Ability to balance a portfolio of applied and long-horizon research
Expertise in governance and review of research programs
Ability to define publication and open-science policy
Solid command of the scientific value and risk of research bets
Expertise in building and scaling research organizations
Ability to set research ethics and safety standards across the organization
Ability to attract and retain leading research talent
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to influence the broader research community
Ability to establish or lead research labs
Familiarity with connecting research output to business strategy
indicators: Defines executive research strategy
Represents the company in the scientific community
Recognized authority in the research area

coreCompetencies: Strategic direction of the organization's research portfolio
Ability to align research investments with long-term company goals
Expertise in technical due diligence of research and its maturity
Ability to set the scientific bar for rigor and reproducibility
Solid command of turning research advances into durable capabilities
Ability to build and lead research organizations
Expertise in balancing scientific ambition with practical delivery
Ability to advise leadership on scientific opportunities and risk
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to shape the data and compute strategy for research
Familiarity with ethics and governance for advanced AI research
Ability to mentor principal researchers and research leads
indicators: Shapes the scientific direction of the organization
Advises leadership on research investments
Mentors senior researchers

coreCompetencies: Executive leadership of the organization's research mission
Ability to align the research program with company strategy
Expertise in planning and managing research budgets
Ability to set policy for responsible and safe research
Solid command of managing the risk of long-horizon research bets
Ability to build scientific reputation and partnerships
Expertise in shaping the organization's research culture at scale
Ability to guide the organization through scientific shifts in the field
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to represent the organization in the scientific community
Familiarity with the funding and collaboration landscape
Ability to shape organizational design for research
indicators: Defines and executes the organization's research vision
Accountable for the outcomes of the research program
Represents the company in the scientific community
